title: Unit Chart Storyboard: One Dataset, Four Layouts (chart.storyboard)

files.name: chart.js
index.html
files.code:
48 startups, four ways to look at them

One cohort of companies, drawn as a unit chart. Scroll the story and the same dots re-cluster, re-bin and resize; each company keeps its identity and glides to its new place. Scroll back up to rewind.

The market

48 startups, one dot each, packed into a single cloud and coloured by sector. This is the whole cohort at a glance.

By sector

The same dots fan out into their sectors. The clusters make it obvious which corners of the market are crowded and which are thin.

By stage

Now regroup by funding stage. Every company travels from its sector to its stage bar, and the classic funnel appears: many seed, few that reach the late rounds.

By capital

Back into one cloud, but now each dot's area is the money it raised. Headcount and capital tell different stories: a handful of late-stage bubbles dwarf the crowd of small rounds.

Startup cohort Beat 1 of 4

Scroll the story, or use the beat dots and Prev/Next. Each beat is a saved view; scrolling back up reverses it.

chart.storyboard.bind({ beats, scroller }) pairs prose with views. Every beat carries the SAME 48 objects with plotOptions.unit.transition: 'identity', which keys each dot by its company name: a specific startup keeps its colour and size and glides across every regroup, re-bin and resize rather than fading out and back in. The unit chart is a premium type; without a license it renders with a trial watermark. Needs the storyboard feature (bundles perspectives).

files.name: styles.css
